_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to read different types of texts using our knowledge of sounds, word meanings and grammar to read accurately and with understanding.

_source.w: We are learning to read different types of texts using our knowledge of sounds, word meanings and grammar to read accurately and with understanding.
y: Combining multiple reading strategies makes us powerful, flexible readers.
Reading accurately and fluently means we can focus on understanding and enjoying texts.
Self-correcting when something does not make sense is what skilled readers do.
eg: We read a new text, using phonics to decode unfamiliar words.
We use the meaning of the sentence to help us work out a word we do not recognise.
We re-read a sentence when it does not make sense and try a different strategy.
We self-correct when we realise a word we read does not fit grammatically.
We read aloud with fluency and expression, showing we understand the text.
We adjust our reading approach for different text types — slower and more careful for complex information.
